label: Freshdesk
description: Arcade.dev LLM tools for Freshdesk customer support

tools.name: AddPrivateNote
tools.description: Add an internal private note to a ticket that the requester cannot see.

parameters.name: ticket_id
parameters.description: The ticket to annotate.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The internal note text. Not visible to the requester.

parameters.name: notify_agent_emails
parameters.description: Agent emails to notify about the note. Defaults to notifying no one.

output.description: Confirmation referencing the created private note.

body.value: Customer has a history of billing disputes. Escalate to Tier 2 support if unresolved within 24 hours.

tools.name: CreateOrUpdateCompany
tools.description:
Create a new company account or update an existing one. To create, omit company_id and
provide a name; link a contact to the result by passing its id as a contact's company.

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description: Id of the company to update. Omit to create a new company instead.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Company name. When creating, this is required and must be unique. When updating, providing it renames the company and omitting it leaves the name unchanged.

parameters.name: domains
parameters.description: Email domains owned by the company, used to auto-associate new contacts. Providing this replaces the existing list; omitting it leaves the domains unchanged.

parameters.name: note
parameters.description: Free-text account note. Providing it sets the note; omitting it leaves it unchanged.

note.value: Enterprise customer since 2021. Renewal due in Q3.

tools.name: CreateOrUpdateContact
tools.description:
Create a new contact or update an existing one. To create, omit contact_id and
provide a name plus at least one of email, phone, or mobile.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Id of the contact to update. Omit to create a new contact instead.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Contact name. When creating, this is required. When updating, providing it changes the name and omitting it leaves the name unchanged.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Contact email. Providing it sets the email; omitting it leaves it unchanged.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Contact phone. Providing it sets the phone; omitting it leaves it unchanged.

parameters.name: mobile
parameters.description: Contact mobile. Providing it sets the mobile; omitting it leaves it unchanged.

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description: Company to associate the contact with. Omitting it leaves it unchanged.

parameters.name: job_title
parameters.description: Contact job title. Providing it sets the title; omitting it leaves it unchanged.

tools.name: CreateTicket
tools.description:
Open a ticket on a customer's behalf. Requires a subject, a description, and a
requester identified by email or id.

parameters.name: subject
parameters.description: The ticket subject line.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: The ticket's first-message body. Plain text or HTML.

parameters.name: requester_email
parameters.description: Email of the contact the ticket is for. Provide this or requester_id. Leave empty when using requester_id.

parameters.name: requester_id
parameters.description: Id of an existing contact the ticket is for. Provide this or requester_email.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Initial status. Defaults to OPEN.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Initial priority. Defaults to MEDIUM.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: Group to assign the new ticket to. Defaults to unassigned.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Agent to assign as owner. Defaults to unassigned.

output.description: The created ticket with its conversation.

subject.value: Unable to reset my account password

description.value:
Hello Support,

I have been trying to reset my password for the past two days but I never receive the reset email. Could you please help me regain access to my account?

Thank you.

tools.name: GetArticle
tools.description:
Read a single knowledge base article's full content by id.

A missing id returns a not-found envelope (``found`` false) rather than raising, so a
stale or guessed id can be recovered from within the same turn.

parameters.name: article_id
parameters.description: The article's unique identifier.

output.description: The article's full content, or a not-found envelope.

tools.name: GetCompany
tools.description:
Read a single company's details by id.

A missing id returns a not-found envelope (``found`` false) rather than raising, so a
stale or guessed id can be recovered from within the same turn.

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description: The company's unique identifier.

output.description: The company's full detail, or a not-found envelope.

tools.name: GetContact
tools.description:
Read a single contact's details by id.

A missing id returns a not-found envelope (``found`` false) rather than raising, so a
stale or guessed id can be recovered from within the same turn.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: The contact's unique identifier.

output.description: The contact's full detail, or a not-found envelope.

tools.name: GetCustomerOverview
tools.description:
Assemble one requester's full context — their contact record, their company, and their
tickets — in a single call instead of three separate lookups.

Identify the requester by contact_id or email; a missing requester returns a not-found
envelope rather than raising, so a stale id or wrong email can be recovered in the same turn.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Id of the requester to profile. Takes precedence over email. Omit to look up by email.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Exact email of the requester to profile. Used only when contact_id is omitted.

parameters.name: include_closed
parameters.description: Include resolved and closed tickets too (true) or only open and pending ones (false). Defaults to false.

parameters.name: ticket_limit
parameters.description: Maximum tickets to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

output.description: The requester's contact, company, and tickets in one envelope.

tools.name: GetTicket
tools.description:
Read a ticket together with its full public-reply and private-note history.

A missing id returns a not-found envelope (``found`` false) rather than raising, so a
stale or guessed id can be recovered from within the same turn.

parameters.name: ticket_id
parameters.description: The ticket's unique identifier.

output.description: The ticket with its conversation, or a not-found envelope.

tools.name: ListAgents
tools.description: List the helpdesk's agents, optionally filtered to one email, for routing tickets.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Exact email to find a single agent by. Leave empty to list all agents.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum agents to return (1-100). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ListGroups
tools.description: List the helpdesk's groups for routing tickets to a team.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum groups to return (1-100). Defaults to 50.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: MergeTickets
tools.description:
Merge duplicate tickets into one. Use this to consolidate related tickets from the same
requester so the conversation lives in a single place; the secondary tickets are closed.

parameters.name: primary_ticket_id
parameters.description: The ticket that survives the merge and absorbs the others.

parameters.name: secondary_ticket_ids
parameters.description: Ids of the duplicate tickets to fold into the primary. Each is closed and its conversation linked into the primary ticket.

parameters.name: note
parameters.description: Internal note added to the primary ticket recording the merge. Leave empty to add no note.

note.value: Merging duplicate tickets submitted by the same requester regarding the login issue reported on 2024-03-15. Tickets #10389, #10412, and #10467 have been consolidated here.

tools.name: ReplyToTicket
tools.description: Post a public reply on a ticket that the requester can see.

parameters.name: ticket_id
parameters.description: The ticket to reply to.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The public reply text the requester will see. Plain text or HTML.

output.description: Confirmation referencing the created public reply.

body.value: Thank you for reaching out to our support team! We have reviewed your issue and are happy to let you know that a fix has been deployed. Please clear your browser cache and try again. If the problem persists, don't hesitate to reply to this ticket and we'll be happy to assist further.

tools.name: SearchArticles
tools.description: Search the knowledge base for published solution articles matching a term.

parameters.name: term
parameters.description: The keyword or phrase to search published articles for.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum articles to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

output.description: Matching published articles with pagination metadata.

tools.name: SearchCompanies
tools.description:
Find companies by name. Match is by name prefix; use the full name for an exact hit.

Returns lightweight summaries without the company note; read a single company by id for
its full detail.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Name or name prefix to match companies by. The name autocomplete requires at least 2 characters; a single-character query returns no matches rather than an error.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum companies to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SearchContacts
tools.description:
Find contacts by name, exact email, or company. With no filters, returns recent
contacts.

Combining a name with a company scans up to the company's first 1000 contacts and matches the
name among them; at a company with more contacts than that, a name match recorded beyond the
scan may not appear.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Name or name prefix to match contacts by. Leave empty to not filter by name. The name autocomplete requires at least 2 characters; a single-character query returns no matches rather than an error.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Exact email to look up a contact by. Leave empty to not filter by email.

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description: Only include contacts at this company. Defaults to no filter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum contacts to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SearchTickets
tools.description:
Find tickets in the queue by status, priority, requester, agent, group, tag,
type, or recency. With no filters, returns the most recently updated tickets.

Pass requester_id (or requester_email) to pull one customer's full ticket history;
other filters then refine that requester's tickets. A just-created ticket may take a
moment to appear here while Freshdesk indexes it.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Only include tickets with this status. Defaults to no filter.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Only include tickets with this priority. Defaults to no filter.

parameters.name: requester_email
parameters.description: Only include tickets opened by the contact with this email. Leave empty for no filter. Ignored when requester_id is given.

parameters.name: requester_id
parameters.description: Only include tickets opened by the contact with this id. Takes precedence over requester_email. Defaults to no filter.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Only include tickets assigned to this agent. Defaults to no filter.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: Only include tickets in this group. Defaults to no filter.

parameters.name: tag
parameters.description: Only include tickets carrying this tag. Leave empty for no filter.

parameters.name: ticket_type
parameters.description: Only include tickets of this type. Leave empty for no filter.

parameters.name: updated_since
parameters.description: Only include tickets updated on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD). Leave empty for no lower bound.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum tickets to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

output.description: Matching tickets, newest first, with pagination metadata.

tools.name: UpdateTicket
tools.description:
Change a ticket's status, priority, assignee, or group. Fields left unset are
unchanged; at least one field must be provided.

parameters.name: ticket_id
parameters.description: The ticket to update.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: New status. Omit to leave the status unchanged.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: New priority. Omit to leave the priority unchanged.

parameters.name: agent_id
parameters.description: Agent to assign as owner. Omit to leave the assignee unchanged.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: Group to move the ticket to. Omit to leave the group unchanged.

output.description: The updated ticket with its conversation.

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description:
Return the authenticated Freshdesk agent's profile.

Use this to verify the configured API key and confirm which Freshdesk account
(domain) the tools are connected to; it is not a required preamble to the other tools.

output.description: The authenticated agent's identity and connected account.

summary:
**Freshdesk** is a customer support platform; this toolkit lets LLM agents read and write Freshdesk data — tickets, contacts, companies, agents, groups, and the knowledge base — without a human clicking through the UI.

## Capabilities

- **Ticket lifecycle** — create, read, update, merge, reply publicly, and add private internal notes to tickets; search the queue by status, priority, requester, agent, group, tag, type, or recency.
- **Contact & company management** — create or update contacts and company accounts; search and retrieve individual records; handle stale or missing IDs gracefully via not-found envelopes rather than hard errors.
- **Customer context assembly** — retrieve a requester's contact record, company, and full ticket history in a single call (`GetCustomerOverview`) instead of three separate lookups.
- **Routing support** — list agents (filterable by email) and groups to assign or escalate tickets to the right team.
- **Knowledge base** — search published solution articles by term and read full article content by ID.
- **Account verification** — confirm the active API key and connected domain with `WhoAmI`.

## Secrets

This toolkit requires two secrets configured in Arcade:

- **`FRESHDESK_API_KEY`** — A per-agent or per-account API key that authenticates all requests. Obtain it from your Freshdesk portal: go to **Profile Settings → API Key** (top-right avatar menu). The key is scoped to the permissions of the agent account it belongs to; use an admin account's key if the toolkit needs to manage agents, groups, or perform bulk operations. See [Freshdesk API authentication docs](https://developers.freshdesk.com/api/#authentication) for details.

- **`FRESHDESK_DOMAIN`** — Your Freshdesk subdomain (e.g., `yourcompany` if your helpdesk URL is `https://yourcompany.freshdesk.com`). This is not a credential but must be supplied so the toolkit knows which Freshdesk instance to target. Find it in the address bar when logged into your Freshdesk account.

Configure both secrets in the Arcade dashboard or via the Arcade secrets API. See the [Arcade secrets guide](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets) and manage secrets at [https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
